_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to describe some differences between stories, information texts and opinion texts.

_source.w: We are learning to describe some differences between stories, information texts and opinion texts.
y: Knowing the difference between text types helps us choose the right one for what we need.
Understanding these differences helps us read each type more effectively.
This also helps us write the right kind of text for our purpose.
eg: We compare a story about a dog with an information text about dogs and note the differences.
We identify whether a text is trying to entertain, inform or persuade us.
We look at the features of each type — stories have characters, information texts have headings, opinion texts have arguments.
We sort a collection of texts into narrative, informative and persuasive groups.
We discuss which type of text we would use to convince our teacher to have a class party.
We create examples of all three types about the same topic — like our school.
